heading: Rules applying to deductions for dividends received
label: 246 Rules applying to deductions for dividends received
notes: Editorial Notes

Amendments2018—Subsec. (e). struck out subsec. (e). Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “No deduction shall be allowed under section 243(a) with respect to a dividend received pursuant to a distribution described in section 936(h)(4).”Pub. L. 115–1412017—Subsec. (a)(1). , substituted “245, and 245A” for “and 245”.Pub. L. 115–97, § 14101(c)(1)Subsec. (b)(1). , which directed amendment of par. Effective Date of 1997 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 105–34, title X, § 1015(c)111 Stat. 922Aug. 5, 1997“(1).—In generalThe amendments made by this section [amending this section] shall apply to dividends received or accrued after the 30th day after the date of the enactment of this Act [].Aug. 5, 1997“(2).—Transitional ruleThe amendments made by this section shall not apply to dividends received or accrued during the 2-year period beginning on the date of the enactment of this Act if—“(A)the dividend is paid with respect to stock held by the taxpayer on , and all times thereafter until the dividend is received,June 8, 1997“(B)such stock is continuously subject to a position described in section 246(c)(4) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 on , and all times thereafter until the dividend is received, andJune 8, 1997“(C)such stock and position are clearly identified in the taxpayer’s records within 30 days after the date of the enactment of this Act.Stock shall not be treated as meeting the requirement of subparagraph (B) if the position is sold, closed, or otherwise terminated and reestablished.”

L. 105–34, title X, § 1015(c)111 Stat. 922Aug. 5, 1997“(1).—In generalThe amendments made by this section [amending this section] shall apply to dividends received or accrued after the 30th day after the date of the enactment of this Act [].Aug. 5, 1997“(2).—Transitional ruleThe amendments made by this section shall not apply to dividends received or accrued during the 2-year period beginning on the date of the enactment of this Act if—“(A)the dividend is paid with respect to stock held by the taxpayer on , and all times thereafter until the dividend is received,June 8, 1997“(B)such stock is continuously subject to a position described in section 246(c)(4) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 on , and all times thereafter until the dividend is received, andJune 8, 1997“(C)such stock and position are clearly identified in the taxpayer’s records within 30 days after the date of the enactment of this Act.Stock shall not be treated as meeting the requirement of subparagraph (B) if the position is sold, closed, or otherwise terminated and reestablished.”

children.heading: Deduction not allowed for dividends from certain corporations
children.label: a Deduction not allowed for dividends from certain corporations

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The deductions allowed by sections 243 245, and 245A shall not apply to any dividend from a corporation which, for the taxable year of the corporation in which the distribution is made, or for the next preceding taxable year of the corporation, is a corporation exempt from tax under section 501 (relating to certain charitable, etc., organizations) or section 521 (relating to farmers’ cooperative associations).1So in original. Probably should be followed by a comma.1

children.heading: Subsection not to apply to certain dividends of Federal Home Loan Banks
children.label: 2 Subsection not to apply to certain dividends of Federal Home Loan Banks

children.heading: Dividends out of current earnings and profits
children.label: A Dividends out of current earnings and profits
i
children.text: the dividends received by the FHLB from the FHLMC during such taxable year, bears to

children.label: ii
children.text: the total earnings and profits of the FHLB for such taxable year.

children.heading: Dividends out of accumulated earnings and profits
children.label: B Dividends out of accumulated earnings and profits
I
children.text: for taxable years ending after , andDecember 31, 1984

children.label: II
children.text: which were not previously treated as distributed under subparagraph (A) or this subparagraph, bears to

children.label: ii
children.text: the total accumulated earnings and profits of the FHLB as of the time such dividend is paid.

children.heading: Coordination with section 243
children.label: C Coordination with section 243
children.text: To the extent that paragraph (1) does not apply to any dividend by reason of subparagraph (A) or (B) of this paragraph, the requirement contained in section 243(a) that the corporation paying the dividend be subject to taxation under this chapter shall not apply.

children.heading: FHLB
children.label: i FHLB
children.text: The term “FHLB” means any Federal Home Loan Bank.

children.heading: FHLMC
children.label: ii FHLMC
children.text: The term “FHLMC” means the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation.

children.heading: Taxable year of FHLB
children.label: iii Taxable year of FHLB
children.text: The taxable year of an FHLB shall, except as provided in regulations prescribed by the Secretary, be treated as the calendar year.

children.label: I
children.text: any dividends received by the FHLB from the FHLMC during such taxable year, and

children.label: II
children.text: the total earnings and profits (determined without regard to dividends described in subclause (I)) of the FHLB as reported in its annual financial statement prepared in accordance with section 20 of the Federal Home Loan Bank Act ().12 U.S.C. 1440

children.heading: Limitation on aggregate amount of deductions
children.label: b Limitation on aggregate amount of deductions

children.heading: General rule
children.label: 1 General rule
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), the aggregate amount of the deductions allowed by section 243(a)(1), subsection (a) and (b) of section 245, and section 250 shall not exceed the percentage determined under paragraph (3) of the taxable income computed without regard to the deductions allowed by sections 172, 199A, 243(a)(1), subsection (a) and (b) of section 245, and 250, without regard to any adjustment under section 1059, and without regard to any capital loss carryback to the taxable year under section 1212(a)(1).2222So in original.2

children.heading: Effect of net operating loss
children.label: 2 Effect of net operating loss
children.text: Paragraph (1) shall not apply for any taxable year for which there is a net operating loss (as determined under section 172).

children.label: A
children.text: first separately with respect to dividends from 20-percent owned corporations (as defined in section 243(c)(2)) and the percentage determined under this paragraph shall be 65 percent, and

children.label: B
children.text: then separately with respect to dividends not from 20-percent owned corporations and the percentage determined under this paragraph shall be 50 percent and the taxable income shall be reduced by the aggregate amount of dividends from 20-percent owned corporations (as so defined).

children.label: A
children.text: which is held by the taxpayer for 45 days or less during the 91-day period beginning on the date which is 45 days before the date on which such share becomes ex-dividend with respect to such dividend, or

children.label: B
children.text: to the extent that the taxpayer is under an obligation (whether pursuant to a short sale or otherwise) to make related payments with respect to positions in substantially similar or related property.

children.heading: 90-day rule in the case of certain preference dividends
children.label: 2 90-day rule in the case of certain preference dividends
A
children.text: by substituting “90 days” for “45 days” each place it appears, and

children.label: B
children.text: by substituting “181-day period” for “91-day period”.

children.label: A
children.text: the day of disposition, but not the day of acquisition, shall be taken into account, and

children.label: B
children.text: paragraph (3) of section 1223 shall not apply.

children.heading: Holding period reduced for periods where risk of loss diminished
children.label: 4 Holding period reduced for periods where risk of loss diminished
A
children.text: the taxpayer has an option to sell, is under a contractual obligation to sell, or has made (and not closed) a short sale of, substantially identical stock or securities,

children.label: B
children.text: the taxpayer is the grantor of an option to buy substantially identical stock or securities, or

children.label: C
children.text: under regulations prescribed by the Secretary, a taxpayer has diminished his risk of loss by holding 1 or more other positions with respect to substantially similar or related property.

children.heading: Special rules for foreign source portion of dividends received from specified 10-percent owned foreign corporations
children.label: 5 Special rules for foreign source portion of dividends received from specified 10-percent owned foreign corporations
I
children.text: by substituting “365 days” for “45 days” each place it appears, and

children.label: II
children.text: by substituting “731-day period” for “91-day period”, and

children.label: ii
children.text: paragraph (2) shall not apply.

children.heading: Status must be maintained during holding period
children.label: B Status must be maintained during holding period
i
children.text: the specified 10-percent owned foreign corporation referred to in section 245A(a) is a specified 10-percent owned foreign corporation at all times during such period, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the taxpayer is a United States shareholder with respect to such specified 10-percent owned foreign corporation at all times during such period.

children.heading: Dividends from a DISC or former DISC
children.label: d Dividends from a DISC or former DISC
children.text: No deduction shall be allowed under section 243 in respect of a dividend from a corporation which is a DISC or former DISC (as defined in section 992(a)) to the extent such dividend is paid out of the corporation’s accumulated DISC income or previously taxed income, or is a deemed distribution pursuant to section 995(b)(1).
